Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query based on specific requirements regarding an event log table named event_log Table Schema case_id Identifier for the process instance activity Name of the activity timestamp Datetime of the event Requirements 1 Identify specific sequence Find cases where the sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order happens in direct succession

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means if we look at the events for a specific case sorted by time event i is Approve event i plus 1 is Create and event i plus 2 is Confirm

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Among the cases identified in step 1 filter further to keep only those where the time difference between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Return all events from the event_log except those belonging to the cases identified in steps 1 and 2

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: To check for direct succession I need to know the next event and the event after that for every row I can use window functions like LEAD or LAG

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LEAD activity 1 over partition by case_id order by timestamp gives the next activity LEAD activity 2 gives the activity after next Similarly LEAD timestamp 1 and LEAD timestamp 2 are needed for the timing condition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to find rows where activity Approve Purchase Requisition Next activity Create Order Next next activity Confirm Order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since I am looking for Approve Purchase Requisition rows Create Order is the next timestamp and Confirm Order is the next next timestamp

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Constructing the filtering logic CTE approach is usually cleanest CTE 1 Add Lead columns CTE 2 Identify bad cases Final Query Select from event_log Where case_id NOT IN Select case_id from CTE 2

Name: Validation - PE
Text: DuckDB syntax specifics LEAD is supported Intervals INTERVAL 5 days NOT IN or NOT EXISTS is easier to read here

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Lets double check the direct succession requirement no other event from the same case in between them This implies sorting strictly by timestamp

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: If timestamps are identical order is ambiguous Usually in process mining unique timestamps or an additional sort key like event_id are preferred

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Given the prompt constraints I will stick to ORDER BY timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The row in question must be Approve Purchase Requisition The next_activity must be Create Order The next_next_activity must be Confirm Order Time difference check next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp greater than INTERVAL 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt asks for time elapsed between Create Order and Confirm Order Create Order is the next event relative to the current row Approve Its timestamp is next_timestamp Confirm Order is the next_next event Its timestamp is next_next_timestamp So the check is next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If any sequence fits the criteria the whole case is excluded

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Does next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp work correctly in DuckDB Yes If columns are timestamps subtraction yields an interval Comparing interval to interval works

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will present the solution clearly with CTEs for readability

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This looks correct and robust for the requirements
